judge_output.rationale: The agent successfully recovered both required findings: (1) Kristian Fredrik Tollefsen Birkeland's death on 23 December 1883 at Stavanger, Rogaland, Norway, and (2) his burial on 31 December 1883 at Stavanger, Rogaland, Norway. Both facts are present in the final tree (person K87G-T89) with correct dates and places. The matching names and identity are confirmed. All required facts were recovered.

proof_quality.rationale: The proof summary is well-structured and addresses the key conflict (the 'H.' middle initial discrepancy in Source 1 vs. Source 2), correctly resolving it in favor of Source 2. The exhaustiveness is partial rather than full: while the agent searched the primary relevant repositories (Norway Church Books, Norway Burials, Probate Index), the agent explicitly notes that the original church book image could not be accessed due to transport limitations, and deferred Find A Grave and Digitalarkivet searches as requiring interactive sessions. The critical weakness is corroboration: both Source 1 and Source 2 derive from the same underlying church burial register entry, making them one evidence unit, not independent sources. The agent acknowledges this ('both entries derive from the same original church burial register and therefore constitute one evidence unit, not two independent sources'). The tier 'probable' is appropriate given this single-source limitation. The narrative is clear and methodical, but the proof rests fundamentally on one underlying record accessed through two derivative transcriptions.

entry.question: When and where did Kristian Fredrik Tollefsen Birkeland (born 1817, Stavanger, Norway) die?
entry.rationale: The project objective requires establishing the death date and place. Norwegian parish death/burial registers (ministerialbok, begravde section) for the relevant parish will record this directly. This is the primary gatekeeper fact — the burial question depends on first locating the death record, which typically names the burial parish as well.

entry.question: In which parish and churchyard was Kristian Fredrik Tollefsen Birkeland buried after his death?
entry.rationale: The project objective explicitly asks where he was buried. Norwegian burial registers record the burial parish and often the specific churchyard. This question is best pursued after (or alongside) the death question, as the same death/burial register entry typically answers both. It depends on q_001 because the burial church is usually the same as or named within the death record.

entry.rationale: The indexed 'Norway, Burials, 1666-1927' collection (CID 1468081) is the fastest entry point for a Stavanger death/burial record. Norwegian burial registers record name, residence, date of death, date of burial, age, occupation, and cause of death — the most direct evidence for the question. Last confirmed record for subject is September 1880; searching for his burial entry here is the highest-probability first step.

'Norway, Church Books, 1797-1958' (CID 4237104) is the most comprehensive indexed Norwegian church-book collection (14.4M records, images attached). The Stavanger Domkirken and Sankt Petri parish burial registers are fully included. This supersedes and cross-checks the legacy Burials collection and should turn up the original church-book entry with image, which confirms both death date and burial parish.

Norway Census 1875 (CID 1529106) — if the subject appears, confirms he was alive in 1875 and establishes his residence, which guides which parish register to search. His children's baptisms were recorded at Stavanger Domkirken through 1860; census will confirm whether the family remained in Stavanger or moved. Absent an 1875 record, it could indicate migration or death before 1875.

Norway Census 1891 (CID 4067726) — absence from this census, combined with presence in 1875 and the last known record in September 1880, would bracket the death to 1880-1891. Presence would extend the window past 1891. A fully indexed and imaged census; his widow's household or any surviving children's households may also provide relational context.

'Norway, Probate Index Cards, 1640-1903' (CID 1661306) — a probate entry would record the death date and provide heirs, confirming identity. Stavanger byfogderi probate records extend to 1903. Useful both as corroborating evidence and as a fallback if church burial records are incomplete for the relevant period.

Digitalarkivet (digitalarkivet.no) — Norway's national digital archive holds church books including burial registers for Stavanger Domkirken and Sankt Petri, sometimes with records not yet indexed on FamilySearch. Also hosts Slekt og Data's Norway Burial Index (1700-2010) at slektogdata.no, which indexes headstones and may confirm burial place. Execute via search-external-sites.

entry.rationale: Name 'Christian Frederick H. Birkeland' is a well-documented Norwegian spelling variant of 'Kristian Fredrik Tollefsen Birkeland': Christian/Kristian and Frederick/Fredrik are standard variant pairs in 19th-century Norwegian records; the patronym 'Tollefsen' is absent (consistent with burial-register practice of recording surname only). The 'H.' middle initial is flagged as a possible indexing artifact but does not undermine identity given the convergence of all other identifiers. Birth year 1817 matches exactly; Stavanger place matches known residence; gender Male matches; record already attached to K87G-T89 in the FamilySearch tree (5-star match). same_person score 0.898 (confidence 5), rank_search_matches score 0.605. Strong match on all key identifiers.
Birth year 1817 corroborates K87G-T89's known birth date of 11 Jul 1817. Combined with name, place, and death/burial date convergence, this is the same person. same_person score 0.898.

Death date 23 Dec 1883 is direct evidence for K87G-T89's death. The name, birth year 1817, and Stavanger location all confirm identity. Last documented record for K87G-T89 is September 1880; a December 1883 death at age 66 is fully consistent. same_person score 0.898. Source is derivative (index); original parish register should be consulted via pli_002 to corroborate.

Burial on 31 Dec 1883 at Stavanger, Rogaland, Norway is direct evidence of burial place for K87G-T89. Identity confirmed by name variant, birth year 1817, and Stavanger location. Note: the index records 'Stavanger, Rogaland, Norway' as burial place but does not name the specific churchyard or parish — pli_002 (Church Books 1797-1958) should supply the specific parish burial register entry.

args.description: Extract pli_002 church book burial record for Birkeland
args.prompt:
Extract all assertions from this FamilySearch church book burial record into the research project.

**Project directory:** `/private/var/folders/tv/yzyl0n517xj5v54fgn0c0j3h0000gn/T/e2e-birkeland-death-1883-jh9kjp00`
**logId:** `log_002` (the log entry for this search already exists — do not create a new one)
**recordId:** `ark:/61903/1:1:68QQ-PP3H`
**Questions this record bears on:** `q_001` (death date/place), `q_002` (burial parish/place)

**Record content (from record_read):**
```json
{
  "persons": [
    {
      "id": "p_292430081798",
      "ark": "ark:/61903/1:1:68QQ-PP3H",
      "gender": "Male",
      "names": [{"type": "BirthName", "surname": "Birkeland", "given": "Christian Frederik"}],
      "facts": [
        {"type": "Birth", "date": "1817", "standard_date": "1817", "place": "Stavanger", "standard_place": "Stavanger, Norway"},
        {"type": "Burial", "primary": true, "date": "31 Dec 1883", "standard_date": "31 Dec 1883", "place": "Stavanger, Rogaland, Norge", "standard_place": "Stavanger, Rogaland, Norway"},
        {"type": "Death", "date": "23 Dec"}
      ],
      "sources": [{"ref": "sd_da1"}]
    }
  ],
  "sources": [
    {"id": "src_r_173859167048", "title": "Entry for Christian Frederik Birkeland, \"Norway Church Books, 1815-1930\"", "citation": "\"Norway Church Books, 1815-1930\", , FamilySearch (https://www.familysearch.org/ark:/61903/1:1:68QQ-PP3H : Fri Mar 08 20:45:47 UTC 2024), Entry for Christian Frederik Birkeland, \"Norway Church Books, 1815-1930\", 31 Dec 1883.", "url": "https://www.familysearch.org/ark:/61903/1:2:4BX2-BDHH"},
    {"id": "sd_c_4237104", "title": "Norway, Church Books, 1797-1958", "url": "https://www.familysearch.org/service/cds/recapi/collections/4237104"},
    {"id": "sd_da1", "url": "https://www.familysearch.org/ark:/61903/3:1:3QHK-93PP-TCSM"}
  ],
  "places": [
    {"id": "2241509", "name": "Stavanger, Rogaland, Norway"},
    {"id": "5939", "name": "Stavanger, Norway"}
  ]
}
```

**Research context:**

The subject is **K87G-T89** — Kristian Fredrik Tollefsen Birkeland, born 11 Jul 1817 in Stavanger, Norway. His wife was Serine Kristoffersdatter Aasland (died 1873). He has multiple children baptised at Stavanger.

This is **pli_002** — the confirmation step for the original church book entry. A derivative index (pli_001, `src_001` already in research.json) found the same burial under "Christian Frederick H. Birkeland" — with a suspect "H." middle initial. This church book record from **Norway, Church Books, 1797-1958** (CID 4237104) records him as **"Christian Frederik Birkeland"** with no "H." middle initial, confirming the "H." was a pli_001 indexing artifact.

**Source classification guidance:**
- The FamilySearch collection "Norway, Church Books, 1797-1958" provides indexed transcriptions WITH attached original images. The image is at `ark:/61903/3:1:3QHK-93PP-TCSM` (sd_da1). The indexed record content we are extracting is a **derivative** of that original image. Classify the source accordingly (derivative), and note that the original image is accessible at the 3:1 ARK.
- The source URL for the record is `https://www.familysearch.org/ark:/61903/1:2:4BX2-BDHH`.

**Death date note:** The death date in the church book is recorded as "23 Dec" — no year is explicitly given in the indexed field. The burial is "31 Dec 1883," and this entry belongs to the 1883 section of the burial register. The death year 1883 is inferred from the burial date and register context. Record the death fact accordingly, noting the year is derived from register context.

**Assertions to create (at minimum):**
1. Name: "Christian Frederik Birkeland" — note this spelling resolves the tentative "H." initial in a_001
2. Birth: 1817, Stavanger
3. Death: 23 Dec [1883 inferred from burial register context], Stavanger
4. Burial: 31 Dec 1883, Stavanger, Rogaland, Norway

**Existing data — do NOT duplicate:**
- `src_001` already exists (derivative: Norway, Burials, 1666-1927) with assertions `a_001`–`a_004` and person_evidence `pe_001`–`pe_004` all linked to K87G-T89. 
- Create a NEW source entry (`src_002`) for this church book record and NEW assertions. Do not modify or supersede the existing ones — they remain valid as independent derivative evidence.
- Do NOT create a new log entry (log_002 already exists for this search).

**After writing assertions, create person_evidence entries** linking each new assertion to K87G-T89. The name match is strong (Christian Frederik Birkeland, birth 1817, Stavanger, burial 31 Dec 1883 — all consistent with K87G-T89). Confidence should be `confident` for the same reasons as pe_001–pe_004.

**Also update pli_002 status to `completed`** once assertions are written (planId: `pl_001`, entryId: `pli_002`).

Corroborates the subject's known birth (11 Jul 1817, Stavanger) already in the tree.

No informant is named in the register for the death date itself (contrast the burial, which the pastor personally conducted); presumed relayed to the pastor by family or others arranging the burial, but this is not stated.

entry.rationale: Name 'Christian Frederik Birkeland' (no 'H.' initial) matches K87G-T89's known naming pattern (Kristian Fredrik Tollefsen Birkeland) even more closely than the src_001 index entry; this closer-to-original church-book record resolves the tentative 'H.' initial as an indexing artifact of src_001. Combined with birth year 1817, Stavanger place, and 31 Dec 1883 burial date convergence with src_001/a_001-a_004, and the record's attachment to K87G-T89 in the FamilySearch tree, identity is confirmed.

Birth year 1817, Stavanger corroborates K87G-T89's documented birth of 11 Jul 1817, Stavanger, and independently corroborates src_001/a_002's 1817 birth year from a different derivative source of the same underlying church register.

Death recorded as 23 Dec (1883 inferred from register context) is consistent with src_001/a_003's 23 Dec 1883 death date from the legacy Burials index, now corroborated by this closer-to-original church-book entry. Name, birth year, and burial date convergence confirm identity with K87G-T89; last documented record for K87G-T89 is September 1880, fully consistent with a December 1883 death.

Burial 31 Dec 1883 at Stavanger, Rogaland, Norway directly answers q_002 (burial parish/place) for K87G-T89, corroborating src_001/a_004 from the legacy Burials index. This church-book entry is a step closer to the original parish register (image attached at ark:/61903/3:1:3QHK-93PP-TCSM, not yet examined) and confirms the burial parish is Stavanger.

4. Any parish or churchyard name in the register header or entry
5. Any additional details: age, cause of death, occupation, residence, informant

Please transcribe the full visible page (all entries) and highlight which entry appears to be Birkeland's.

entry.narrative_markdown:
# Death of Kristian Fredrik Tollefsen Birkeland (born 1817, Stavanger, Norway)

## Research Question

When and where did Kristian Fredrik Tollefsen Birkeland (born 11 July 1817, Stavanger, Norway; FamilySearch person K87G-T89) die?

## Evidence

### Norwegian Church Burial Register

Norway did not mandate civil registration of deaths until 1916. For a death in Stavanger in 1883, the church burial register is the primary — and effectively the only — contemporaneous vital record. Two separate FamilySearch database entries, each derived from the same underlying church burial register, record the death and burial of a person identified as this subject.

**Source 1.** The "Norway, Burials, 1666–1927" collection (FamilySearch, CID 1468081) contains a derivative index entry for "Christian Frederick H. Birkeland," recording death 23 December 1883, burial 31 December 1883, Stavanger, Rogaland, Norway, birth year 1817.[1] The "H." middle initial is addressed under Discrepancy below.

**Source 2.** The "Norway, Church Books, 1797–1958" collection (FamilySearch, CID 4237104) contains a second indexed entry for "Christian Frederik Birkeland," recording burial 31 December 1883 at Stavanger, Rogaland, Norway, birth year 1817 at Stavanger.[2] The death date is given as "23 Dec" without an explicit year; that year is inferred from the burial date and the register's 1883 temporal context. An attached page-scan image (ark:/61903/3:1:3QHK-93PP-TCSM) was sought via the image-reader agent but could not be read — the file is 0.8 MB, over the MCP transport cap — and remains unexamined.

Both entries derive from the same original church burial register and therefore constitute **one evidence unit**, not two independent sources. They agree on the death date (23 December 1883) and burial place (Stavanger, Rogaland, Norway).

### Identity of the Subject

The "Christian Frederik Birkeland" of the burial entries is identified as Kristian Fredrik Tollefsen Birkeland (K87G-T89) on the following grounds: (1) the name is a well-documented Dano-Norwegian spelling variant — *Christian/Kristian* and *Frederick/Frederik* are standard alternates in 19th-century Norwegian records, and the patronymic *Tollefsen* does not appear on burial register entries, which record the hereditary surname only; (2) birth year 1817 matches the subject's known birth date of 11 July 1817; (3) Stavanger is his lifelong residence; and (4) a FamilySearch same-person match score of 0.898 corroborates the identification. The record was already attached to K87G-T89 in the FamilySearch tree.

### Discrepancy: "H." Middle Initial

Source 1 records the name as "Christian Frederick H. Birkeland." No middle initial appears in any other known record for this individual. Source 2 — drawn from the "Norway, Church Books, 1797–1958" collection, which links to the original register image — transcribes the name as "Christian Frederik Birkeland" without any middle initial. This discrepancy is resolved: the "H." is a transcription artifact introduced in the legacy "Norway, Burials, 1666–1927" indexing project, not a name the subject used. Source 2 is preferred on this point.

### Probate Search — Negative Evidence

The "Norway, Probate Index Cards, 1640–1903" (FamilySearch, CID 1661306) was searched for both "Christian Birkeland" and "Kristian Birkeland"; no record was found.[3] The collection does index 53 Birkeland entries from across Norway, including several from Rogaland, confirming adequate coverage. The absence of a probate record does not contradict the established death date; estates in 1880s Norway were frequently handled informally or are absent from this particular index.

## Conclusion

The preponderance of available evidence strongly suggests that Kristian Fredrik Tollefsen Birkeland died on **23 December 1883** in Stavanger, Rogaland, Norway, at approximately age 66. This conclusion is rated **probable**: the evidence rests on a single evidence unit (the church burial register) accessed through two derivative transcriptions; no independent source was found, and the original church book image is inaccessible. Should the original image become readable, or should an independent record (probate, newspaper obituary) be located, the tier could advance to proved.

---

[1] "Norway Burials, 1666–1927," database, FamilySearch (https://familysearch.org/ark:/61903/1:2:MX9C-7QV : accessed 2026-07-12), entry for Christian Frederick H. Birkeland, 1883; derivative source, secondary information, direct evidence.

[2] "Norway, Church Books, 1815–1930," database, FamilySearch (https://www.familysearch.org/ark:/61903/1:2:4BX2-BDHH : accessed 2026-07-12), entry for Christian Frederik Birkeland, 31 Dec 1883; derivative source, secondary information for death date (primary for burial), direct evidence; original image (ark:/61903/3:1:3QHK-93PP-TCSM) not read — transport-size limitation.

[3] "Norway, Probate Index Cards, 1640–1903," database, FamilySearch, CID 1661306, searched 2026-07-12; no result for Christian or Kristian Birkeland.

entry.exhaustive_search_summary: Same searches as q_001 (pl_001 — Norway Burials 1666-1927, Norway Church Books 1797-1958, probate). The specific parish and churchyard within Stavanger are not identified in either indexed record. The original church book image (ark:/61903/3:1:3QHK-93PP-TCSM), which would name the recording parish, was pursued via image-reader agent but is inaccessible (transport limitation). No headstone database (Slektogdata.no, Find A Grave) was searched; such a search might identify the specific churchyard but was not within the scope of the primary FamilySearch plan.
entry.narrative_markdown:
# Burial of Kristian Fredrik Tollefsen Birkeland (died 23 December 1883)

## Research Question

In which parish and churchyard was Kristian Fredrik Tollefsen Birkeland (FamilySearch person K87G-T89; died 23 December 1883) buried?

## Evidence

The same two derivative index entries from the church burial register that establish the death date (see proof summary ps_001) also record the burial. Both are derived from the same original church burial register entry and constitute a single evidence unit.

**Source 1.** "Norway, Burials, 1666–1927" (FamilySearch, CID 1468081) records burial of "Christian Frederick H. Birkeland" on 31 December 1883 at Stavanger, Rogaland, Norway.[1] This is a derivative index; the burial-date entry carries primary information in the sense that a church official documented the burial as part of official duty, though the specific churchyard is not recorded in the indexed fields.

**Source 2.** "Norway, Church Books, 1797–1958" (FamilySearch, CID 4237104) records burial of "Christian Frederik Birkeland" on 31 December 1883 at Stavanger, Rogaland, Norway.[2] The burial fact carries primary information — the church official recording the burial did so as a direct participant in an official capacity. Again, the indexed record gives only "Stavanger, Rogaland, Norway" without naming the specific parish or churchyard.

Both sources agree on burial date (31 December 1883) and burial place (Stavanger, Rogaland, Norway). No conflict exists on these points.

## Limitation: Specific Parish and Churchyard Unknown

In 1883, Stavanger contained multiple parishes and churchyards, including Stavanger Domkirken (the cathedral) and Sankt Petri. The recording parish would normally appear in the header of the original register page. The original church book image (ark:/61903/3:1:3QHK-93PP-TCSM) was sought via the image-reader agent but could not be read due to a transport-size limitation. As a result, the specific parish that recorded the burial — and therefore the specific churchyard — cannot be identified from available indexed evidence.

A headstone or memorial database search (Slektogdata.no, Find A Grave) was not conducted; such sources sometimes identify the specific churchyard and could be pursued to add specificity to this conclusion.

## Conclusion

The preponderance of available evidence strongly suggests that Kristian Fredrik Tollefsen Birkeland was buried on **31 December 1883** in **Stavanger, Rogaland, Norway**. The specific parish and churchyard within Stavanger cannot be determined from accessible indexed evidence. This conclusion is rated **probable**: the burial date and city are consistently recorded across two derivatives of a single evidence unit; the original image is inaccessible; and the specific churchyard is not identified in any searched source.

---

[1] "Norway Burials, 1666–1927," database, FamilySearch (https://familysearch.org/ark:/61903/1:2:MX9C-7QV : accessed 2026-07-12), entry for Christian Frederick H. Birkeland, 1883; derivative source, primary information for burial, direct evidence.

[2] "Norway, Church Books, 1815–1930," database, FamilySearch (https://www.familysearch.org/ark:/61903/1:2:4BX2-BDHH : accessed 2026-07-12), entry for Christian Frederik Birkeland, 31 Dec 1883; derivative source, primary information for burial (official duty), direct evidence; original image not read — transport-size limitation.

args.prompt:
Please review the proof summaries for research project `rp_birkeland_death_1883` and provide your GPS critique.

**Project directory:** `/private/var/folders/tv/yzyl0n517xj5v54fgn0c0j3h0000gn/T/e2e-birkeland-death-1883-jh9kjp00`

**Research objective:** When and where did Kristian Fredrik Tollefsen Birkeland (born 11 July 1817, Stavanger, Norway; FamilySearch K87G-T89) die, and where was he buried?

**Proof summaries written (both at `probable` tier):**

**ps_001 — q_001 (death date/place):**
- Tier: probable, vehicle: summary
- Evidence: Two derivative FamilySearch index entries (Norway, Burials, 1666–1927 [src_001] and Norway, Church Books, 1797–1958 [src_002]) both record Christian Frederik Birkeland, death 23 Dec 1883, burial 31 Dec 1883, Stavanger, Rogaland, Norway, born 1817.
- Both entries derive from the same underlying church burial register — ONE evidence unit.
- Original church book image (ark:/61903/3:1:3QHK-93PP-TCSM) was pursued via image-reader agent but returned NOT READ (0.8 MB over MCP transport cap) — documented as pursued-and-unavailable.
- Probate (Norway Probate Index Cards 1640-1903, CID 1661306) searched for Christian/Kristian Birkeland — negative.
- Norwegian civil death registration began 1916; the church burial register is the only contemporaneous vital record type for 1883.
- Name discrepancy ("H." middle initial in src_001) investigated and resolved as legacy-index transcription artifact confirmed by src_002.
- Identity: same_person score 0.898, name variant (Christian Frederik = Kristian Fredrik), birth 1817 match, Stavanger match.

**ps_002 — q_002 (burial parish/churchyard):**
- Tier: probable, vehicle: summary
- Same evidence as ps_001. Burial 31 Dec 1883, Stavanger recorded.
- Specific parish/churchyard within Stavanger NOT identified in indexed records (only "Stavanger, Rogaland, Norway").
- Original image inaccessible (same transport cap issue).
- No headstone database (Slektogdata, FindAGrave) searched.

Please read the full `research.json` to review the complete evidence set, and provide your assessment of:
1. Whether the `probable` tier is correctly calibrated for both conclusions
2. Whether the research scope was sufficient (GPS Component 1)
3. Whether the evidence analysis and source classification is sound (GPS Component 3)
4. What, if anything, would need to be done to advance either conclusion to `proved`
5. Any other issues a BCG-level reviewer would flag

Keep your critique concise and actionable.

Search URL: https://www.findagrave.com/memorial/search?firstname=Christian&lastname=Birkeland&birthyear=1817&deathyear=1883&location=Stavanger%2C+Norway. Searching for Christian/Kristian Birkeland (born 1817, died 23 Dec 1883, Stavanger, Norway) to identify the specific churchyard within Stavanger. GPS mentor (ev_002) flagged this as address_first for ps_002. Curated FamilySearch link for FindAGrave Norway points to country-level search only (no Stavanger-specific cemetery index found). Also note: gravminne.no (Norwegian grave memorial database) is an additional avenue not yet searched. An interactive session should execute this search and update ps_002 accordingly.

fields.exhaustive_search_summary: Same searches as q_001 (pl_001 — Norway Burials 1666-1927, Norway Church Books 1797-1958, probate). The specific parish and churchyard within Stavanger are not identified in either indexed record. The original church book image (ark:/61903/3:1:3QHK-93PP-TCSM), which would name the recording parish, was pursued via image-reader agent but is inaccessible (transport limitation). Find A Grave searched (deferred — autonomous run; URL: https://www.findagrave.com/memorial/search?firstname=Christian&lastname=Birkeland&birthyear=1817&deathyear=1883&location=Stavanger%2C+Norway; logged as log_004). Digitalarkivet burial search (https://www.digitalarkivet.no/en/search/79) and gravminne.no not yet executed — require interactive user session. GPS mentor (ev_002) flagged the churchyard gap as address_first; these deferred searches are the recommended follow-up for an interactive session. The burial-place conclusion ('Stavanger, Rogaland, Norway') is supported at probable by the church register evidence; the specific churchyard cannot be determined from searches conducted in this autonomous run.
